steps.description: Audit and correct the existing backend file upload implementation so it exactly matches the requested API contract and does not reintroduce the prior verification/type/runtime errors: align env usage, response shapes, local download URLs, UUID validation, static uploads serving, multipart handling expectations, and route mounting behavior.

Audit and correct the comments backend routes so they satisfy the requested thread/list/create/reply/update/resolve/delete semantics and also match the rest of the app/test contract, including permission checks, response envelopes, UUID validation, resolved_at handling, and route mounting under both /api/documents and /api/comments without breaking CSRF/auth behavior.

Audit and correct the backlinks backend routes so GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks and POST /api/documents/:id/links follow the requested contract, preserve current visibility behavior, and reconcile schema/test mismatches such as payload keys, return fields, ordering, and any issue display/type metadata expected by existing consumers/tests.

Fix frontend upload service and tracker code to match the backend contract and remove the likely source of the prior introduced errors: normalize request/response typing, ensure direct S3 PUT vs local multipart POST flows work, provide usable progress tracking APIs, and correct navigation-warning tracker exports/imports and state shape.
Fix editor upload integrations so image/file embedding uses the corrected upload service/tracker APIs and the resulting URLs render correctly in TipTap for both local dev and presigned S3 uploads.

Fix comments frontend query hooks to match the actual backend request/response contract and current editor comment-thread model, including thread creation, replying via parent_id/comment_id, update/resolve/delete mutations, cache updates, and any missing exported helpers used by comment display code.

Reconcile comment mark/editor integration with the corrected comments hook contract so new inline comment threads can be created from TipTap selections and rendered/resolved without type or runtime mismatches.

Wire global app rendering for upload navigation warnings if not already correctly mounted after the tracker fixes, ensuring the warning component is actually rendered in the authenticated app tree without breaking existing providers/layout.

   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP

   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;

   169	DECLARE

   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;

Implement file upload and inline comments systems for Ship.

For file uploads, create `routes/files.ts` with auth middleware: POST /api/files/upload-request (validates filename/mimeType/sizeBytes with Zod, blocks dangerous extensions like .exe/.bat/.sh/.dll/.jar/.ps1, creates a files row with status=pending, returns a presigned S3 upload URL if S3_UPLOADS_BUCKET is configured or a local upload endpoint for dev), POST /api/files/upload/:id (for local dev: accepts multipart form data, writes to uploads/ directory, updates files row to status=uploaded), GET /api/files/:id (returns file metadata + download URL). For S3: use @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner with 15-minute URL expiration. For local dev: serve uploads/ as static files. Set max file size to 1GB. Validate UUID format on all ID params to prevent path traversal.

For comments, create `routes/comments.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/comments (list all comment threads for a document, grouped by comment_id), POST /api/documents/:id/comments (create a new comment thread with comment_id matching the TipTap comment mark, or reply to existing via parent_id), PATCH /api/comments/:id (update comment content), POST /api/comments/:id/resolve (set resolved_at timestamp), DELETE /api/comments/:id (only author or admin can delete). Create `routes/backlinks.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks (query document_links where target_id matches, return source documents with title and type), POST /api/documents/:id/links (upsert document_links from extracted mentions/links in content).

On the frontend, create `src/services/upload.ts` with: requestUpload (calls upload-request endpoint), uploadFile (either uploads directly to presigned S3 URL or to the local endpoint), trackUploadProgress. Create `src/services/uploadTracker.ts` to track active uploads and warn on navigation (UploadNavigationWarning component). Create `src/hooks/useCommentsQuery.ts` for fetching and mutating comments via TanStack Query.

Verify: upload an image via the API, confirm it returns a valid URL, embed it in a TipTap document via the editor, confirm it renders. Create a comment thread on a document, reply to it, resolve it, confirm the resolved_at timestamp is set.
